THE CASTINGFormat Pitch Deck Page 01 / 03 — Title & Hook

Returning Format · Peacock / Bravo

The Casting

The audition process reality TV has never shown you — real creators run the real casting gauntlet, competing not for a trophy but for actual jobs on the network’s next show.

8 × 60′ per season Structured-reality competition Franchise engine — new genre every season
THE CASTINGFormat Pitch Deck Page 02 / 03 — The Format

02The Format

Structure

8 episodes, one funnel stage each. EP1 open call narrows thousands of applicants to a shortlist. EP2–7: Chemistry Reads, The Wired House, Confessional Tests, Mock Network Pitch, Focus Group, Role-Breakdown Recall — each a genuine cut, scored on camera. EP8: contingent offers, revealed as the downstream show’s first-look trailer.

Access & Cast

A recurring panel of working reality-TV casting directors plus the network’s own current-programming exec — judging on tradecraft alone through Stage 5, with network authority at the final cut only. Contestants are real creators (50k–2M followers), sourced by open call — no pre-cast names, no plants.

Tone

Transparent, wry and self-aware about reality TV’s own machinery — but warm toward its contestants. Demystifies the process; never mocks the people in it.

Episode to Episode

Opens on thousands of hopefuls and a hashtag challenge; closes on a live, scored elimination with a real job on the line. Every stage displays the actual scoring criteria on screen — the audience learns the craft alongside the cast, and the field always narrows by more finalists than there are jobs.

The Casting — Format Returning · Structured-Reality
THE CASTINGFormat Pitch Deck Page 03 / 03 — Why Now & Why Us

03Why Now, Why Us

The Cultural Hook

Streamers are already converting followings into cast contracts — Alix Earle’s new Netflix series, Peacock’s Bravo-talent slate, a ~30% rise in creator-to-reality deals — but the casting process itself has never been the show. That headline will be common knowledge by air date, so we’re not resting on it: real casting-director tradecraft stays closed-door regardless of how mainstream the pipeline becomes — the durable hook outlasts the trend piece.

Comparables (reference only)
The ApprenticeHell’s KitchenFood Network StarScream QueensAmerica’s Next Top ModelThe CircleLove Island
Every comp tests one skill in one genre. This is built on the casting director’s toolkit itself — reskinned each season to whatever show is being cast next.
Target & Audience

Peacock / Bravo — flagship, signature-launch format for Bravo’s marquee unscripted block or a Peacock season drop. Bravo/Peacock’s core unscripted audience, skewing female-leaning 18–49, plus the built-in social audience of every creator who enters.
